channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: GOP-led Senate confirming president's nominees at faster pace than Biden admin, first Trump term
item.description: President Donald Trump's nominees for his positions in his second term are shaping up quicker than his first term and former President Joe Biden's.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Execution of man convicted of killing pastor in 2011 scheduled Wednesday
item.description: Steven Lawayne Nelson has maintained that while he participated unknowingly in a violent church robbery, he did not harm anyone.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Ayden Runnels, KPRC 2

item.title: California Gov. Newsom to seek more federal funds for LA fire recovery during DC meeting with Trump
item.description: Gov. Gavin Newsom departed California on Tuesday to meet with President Donald Trump and members of Congress to discuss federal disaster aid following the devastating wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump executive order forces New Jersey to cancel its first offshore wind farm
item.description: President Donald Trump’s executive order banning offshore wind projects has brought New Jersey’s first offshore wind project to a standstill.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's proposed US takeover of Gaza Strip elicits positive response across administration
item.description: President Trump says the U.S. will "take over the Gaza Strip," after meeting with Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu. Now, leaders are taking to social media to express their support..
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran praises US for cutting foreign aid funding as it looks for a Trump message on nuclear talks
item.description: Iran’s government seems to be welcoming decisions by the United States — even though they happen to come from a man Iranian operatives have allegedly been plotting to assassinate.
item.dc:creator: Nasser Karimi And Jon Gambrell, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump says he's given advisers instructions for Iran to be 'obliterated' if it assassinates him
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Tuesday that he's given his advisers instructions to obliterate Iran if it assassinates him. “If they did that they would be obliterated,” Trump said in an exchange with reporters while signing an executive order calling for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, AAMER MADHANI and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada prisons director points to 4-mile fence, better staffing; inmate food issues persist
item.description: A high-profile inmate escape, the resignation of the prisons director, and reports that fences, doors and windows were vulnerable. It all added up to a spotlight on the Nevada Department of Corrections in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gabbard, RFK Jr. pass committee hurdles in win for Trump
item.description: Tulsi Gabbard and Robert F. Kennedy Jr. on Tuesday cleared crucial hurdles toward winning Senate confirmation, handing President Trump a key win as he looks for an early display of loyalty from GOP lawmakers.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Trump will attend the Super Bowl in New Orleans. He'll be the first sitting president to go
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump is going to the Super Bowl in New Orleans on Sunday, the first sitting president to do so. The U.S. Secret Service, which is responsible for protecting the president, said in a statement Tuesday that it has had staff on the ground for days preparing for his visit.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard, Trump's pick to oversee US spy agencies, clears Senate committee
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Tulsi Gabbard's nomination to be President Donald Trump's director of national intelligence cleared a key Senate committee Tuesday despite concerns raised about her past comments sympathetic to Russia and a meeting with Syria’s now-deposed leader.
item.dc:creator: DAVID KLEPPER and FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate panel advances Tulsi Gabbard nomination for DNI
item.description: The Senate Intelligence Committee on Tuesday advanced Tulsi Gabbard’s nomination to become the next director of national intelligence (DNI), putting her in position to get confirmed by the full chamber after key senators threw their support behind her. The 9-8 vote was along party lines.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Agents sue FBI, DOJ amid fears of retaliation over work on Jan. 6 cases
item.description: The FBI and the Department of Justice (DOJ) are being sued by 16 current agents who say their review of those who worked on the cases of some 1,500 Jan. 6 defendants is a preparation to retaliate against agents.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: Musk: Trump 'will succeed' in dismantling Education Department
item.description: “Reagan campaigned on ending the federal Dept of Education, which was created by Carter in 1979, but it was bigger when Reagan left office than when he started!” Musk said.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: How Trump’s pledge to punish South Africa reflects Musk’s criticism of his homeland
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump says he will cut all funding to South Africa over what he called a massive human rights violation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Schools poised to be major front line in Trump immigration crackdown
item.description: Oklahoma is moving to require proof of citizenship for the guardians of public school students, while California schools are distributing "know your rights" cards to immigrant families as K-12 campuses become a crucial front line in President Trump's immigration crackdown.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: What USAID does, and why Trump and Musk want to get rid of it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Dozens of senior officials put on leave. Thousands of contractors laid off. A freeze put on billions of dollars in humanitarian assistance to other countries. Over the last two weeks, President Donald Trump's administration has made significant changes to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: ELLEN KNICKMEYER and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways on Musk, Trump war with USAID
item.description: President Donald Trump and billionaire tech entrepreneur Elon Musk have moved to shut down the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID), which spent some $30 billion last year on humanitarian, development and security aid to more than 100 countries.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, Fox 5

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Dozens of employees with the Department of Education were placed on administrative leave on Friday, allegedly after attending a diversity training course encouraged by then-Secretary Betsy DeVos during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Who gets more disaster aid? Republican states. Here's the breakdown
item.description: Experts say every state gets millions in federal help — and a little more goes to Republican states and congressional districts.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Marco Rubio taking over as acting head of USAID amid agency turmoil
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is being put in charge of the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) as the Trump administration mulls the future of the agency.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Florida immigration bill, marijuana petition spark debate
item.description: The battle between Gov. Ron DeSantis and state GOP lawmakers has become more public and continues to heat up.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump signs tariffs on Canada, Mexico and China
item.description: "Tariffs on imports from Canada, Mexico, and China are SIGNED!" White House spokesperson Harrison Fields posted on the social media platform X.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, PIX 11

item.title: Ken Martin elected next DNC chair
item.description: Minnesota state party chair Ken Martin was elected on Saturday as the next leader of the Democratic National Committee (DNC) after securing a majority of the 448 DNC committee members’ votes.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, WFLA, NBC

item.title: New York expands efforts to stop human trafficking
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – New York is expanding its efforts to stop human trafficking. Gov. Kathy Hochul recently signed a series of bills to shed light on the issue, which targets young women. The NYPD's human trafficking task force conducted more than 300 operations in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: President Trump casts blame during briefing on deadly plane crash
item.description: President Donald Trump started Thursday’s press briefing with a moment of silence for the victims and their families after a deadly crash involving an American Airlines plane and an Army helicopter near Ronald Reagan National Airport on Wednesday night.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Connell, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans leave Florida frustrated with lack of final plan to pass Trump agenda
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) arrived at this week’s House Republican retreat with hopes of uniting the fractious GOP conference around a plan to pass President Trump's agenda — but instead, the group is departing south Florida with rising tensions in its ranks. Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene (R-Ga.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, Fox 5

item.title: DeSantis signals veto of TRUMP Act, criticizing lawmakers' immigration bill
item.description: After a late night for lawmakers in Tallahassee, Governor Ron DeSantis says his veto pen is ready to reject the TRUMP Act.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democrats demand Patel information in special counsel's classified documents report
item.description: Democrats on the Senate Judiciary Committee are demanding to see the volume of former special counsel Jack Smith’s report dealing with the Mar-a-Lago classified documents investigation, asserting it has relevant information about Kash Patel, President Trump’s pick to lead the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: White House budget office rescinds federal funding freeze memo
item.description: The Trump administration’s Office of Management and Budget released a new memo Wednesday rescinding a controversial memo issued late Monday that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance, which had paralyzed many federal programs and caused a huge uproar on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5
